title: Energy plant operatives
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 26 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Take readings and record data, such as water levels, temperatures, or flow rates.
Receive instructions from steam engineers regarding steam plant and air compressor operations.
Switch from automatic to manual controls and isolate equipment mechanically and electrically for safe inspection and repair work.
Check air quality of ventilation systems, make adjustments, and monitor equipment to ensure compliance with safety codes and reliable operation.
Clean and lubricate boilers and auxiliary equipment, making minor adjustments as needed using hand tools.
Analyze problems in equipment operations and take appropriate action to ensure reliable operation of systems.
Monitor boiler water, chemical, and fuel levels, adjusting as needed to maintain required levels and conditions.
Test boiler water quality or arrange for testing and take necessary corrective action such as adding chemicals.
Observe and interpret readings on gauges and meters to ensure boiler operations are normal.
Operate stationary engines, boilers, and auxiliary equipment to supply steam or heat for various applications.
Inspect equipment for leaks or malfunctions, and ensure that systems are operating efficiently and safely.
Clean and lubricate parts, perform safety checks, and repair or replace components as necessary to maintain equipment.
Monitor oil, temperature, and pressure gauges to ensure engines operate safely and efficiently.
Drive machines into piles of material and operate levers to move conveyor booms or shovels for material handling.
Maintain records of operational data and materials moved using specified forms to track compliance and efficiency.
Clean hoppers and remove spillage from tracks and conveyor decking to maintain operational safety and efficiency.
Examine sketches, diagrams, blueprints, or photographs to decide how designs are to be etched, cut, or engraved onto workpieces.
Design and oversee testing, installation, and repair of marine apparatus and equipment; maintain and coordinate repair of marine machinery.
Evaluate operation of marine equipment during acceptance testing and shakedown cruises, including conducting environmental, operational, or performance tests on marine machinery.
Prepare technical reports for engineering, management, or sales personnel; and write performance requirements for product development or engineering projects.
Investigate equipment failures or difficulties to diagnose faulty operation and recommend remedial actions.
Evaluate product data or design from inspections or reports for conformance to engineering principles and quality standards.
Design and supervise environmental projects in agriculture, manage construction of rural electric-power distribution, and irrigation systems.
Provide technical customer service and discuss plans with clients, contractors, and other engineers to evaluate modifications and ensure project needs are met.
Design complete hull and superstructure of marine vessels according to specifications and safety standards.
Analyze project requests and engineering data to determine feasibility and costs for aerospace or aeronautical products.
Maintain records of engineering department activities, including expense records, maintenance, and repair details.
Conduct research that tests or analyzes the feasibility, design, operation, or performance of equipment, components, or systems.
Confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations and activities within or between departments.
Evaluate employee performance and recommend or implement personnel actions, such as hirings or promotions.
Plan and develop new products and production processes, and plan work assignments to meet production goals.
Calculate labor and equipment requirements and production specifications, using standard formulas.
Set up, adjust, and maintain machines and equipment to ensure proper operation.
Requisition materials, supplies, equipment parts, or repair services as needed for operations.
Determine production standards, budgets, goals, and rates based on policies and resource availability.
Direct and coordinate activities of employees engaged in production or processing of goods.
Read and analyze charts, work orders, and production schedules to evaluate current production estimates and requirements.
Maintain operations data and prepare management reports on production results.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, and company policies for workers and explain work tasks to new workers.
Observe work and monitor gauges to ensure operators conform to production standards.
Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions and ensure conformance to safety standards.
Conduct and assign employee training in equipment operations or work and safety procedures.
Enforce safety rules and regulations to maintain a safe working environment.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, or work-related accidents.
Resolve worker problems or complaints in collaboration with employees and management.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to assist workers and complete work assignments.
Participate in continuing education to stay informed of industry trends and developments.
